title: Iron Snake Boards with Interior Holes
content:
## Overview

Iron Snake boards can contain interior holes — voids punched into the playable area that the snake must navigate around. Colliding with a hole is fatal, identical to hitting the outer wall. This feature was added at customer request (JOE-6).

## Implementation

### `carveHoles()` — `src/iron-snake.ts`

A `carveHoles()` function punches small interior voids into an already-valid solid board shape. Before any hole is committed, it is validated against two criteria:

1. **Single-component connectivity** — the remaining playable area must stay fully connected, ensuring all food positions remain reachable.
2. **Minimum-width clearance** — the same morphological open used to guard the base shape is reused here. Any hole that would create a corridor narrower than the minimum-width threshold is rejected. This automatically prevents thin corridors and edge bites without additional code.

Both validators are reused verbatim from the base shape generator.

### Integration with board generators

Hole carving is wired into both board generators:

- **`generateIronSnakeBoard`** (level 1 boards): carves holes after the solid shape passes its own validation.
- **`growIronSnakeBoard`** (level-transition boards): builds a protected-cell set covering every current snake cell plus its orthogonal neighbors before carving. This guarantees a hole can never open beneath a snake or immediately in front of a head when play resumes.

### Placement strategy

Each hole placement attempt is anchored on a known playable cell (rather than a random position across the full bounding grid). The entire hole footprint must be surrounded by playable neighbors — the "interior only" constraint. This approach was chosen because the playable blob covers only ~57% of the bounding grid, making unanchored random attempts wasteful and unreliable, especially on small early boards where deep-interior positions are a minority.

### Difficulty scaling

Hole count and maximum footprint size scale with the Iron Snake level:

| Stage | Holes | Max footprint |
|---|---|---|
| Early levels | 1–2 | Single-cell gaps |
| Level 8–10 | Several | Up to ~4×4 cells |

This provides a natural difficulty ramp without a separate tuning pass. Scaling is tied directly to the current level so the difficulty curve is automatic as the arena grows.

## Files

- `src/iron-snake.ts` — `carveHoles()` function and integration into both board generators
- `src/main.ts` — wiring changes
